[title] [bug] `/stocks/ca/ticker aapl/get` - error: list index out of range.
[issue]
```
(🦋) /stocks/ca/ $ ticker aapl

(🦋) /stocks/ca/ $ get

list index out of range
[finviz] similar companies: aapl,

(🦋) /stocks/ca/ $

```

the other two sources work:

```
(🦋) /stocks/ca/ $ get --source polygon

[polygon] similar companies: aapl, msft, nok, ibm, hpq, googl, bb, xlk

(🦋) /stocks/ca/ $ get --source finnhub

[finnhub] similar companies: aapl, dell, hpq, 1337.hk, hpe, ntap, wdc, smci, pstg, xrx
```
[issue_comments] "is an issue with the finvizfinance library:

```
file "mocking_user_path/openbbterminal/openbbterminal/openbb_terminal/stocks/comparison_analysis/finviz_compare_model.py", line 44, in get_similar_companies\n    similar = overview().compare(symbol, compare_list, verbose=0)\n  file "mocking_user_path/miniconda3/envs/obb/lib/python3.9/site-packages/finvizfinance/screener/overview.py", line 328, in compare\n    stock_fundament = stock.ticker_fundament()\n  file "mocking_user_path/miniconda3/envs/obb/lib/python3.9/site-packages/finvizfinance/quote.py", line 141, in ticker_fundament\n    ) = rows[2].text.split(" | "
```", "fixed by updating to the pre-release:

```
pip install finvizfinance==0.14.6rc2
```

editing in release branch"
reasoning_claude: This is a third-party library version incompatibility issue that was resolved by updating the external dependency. Automating tests for transient library bugs that are fixed upstream provides no long-term value and would only add maintenance burden.
evidence_claude: is an issue with the finvizfinance library... fixed by updating to the pre-release: pip install finvizfinance==0.14.6rc2

[title] [bug] `account\list` displays `no routines found.` but opens a windows with 3 default routines
[issue]
**describe the bug**
i have not set any routine in my account. so executing `accounts\list` command displays the proper output -

however, it opens a window that displays 3 routines configured -
[issue_comments] "on my.openbb.dev profile, i see these as default routines -

then the output should not be `no routines found.`. may i know what's the expected output for this command so that i can develop a fix for it?", "thanks for this issue, @the-praxs!  this is because the routines are not refreshed in memory and you need to restart in order for them to be recognized.  the choices for the `exe` command need to refresh after.", "> thanks for this issue, @the-praxs! this is because the routines are not refreshed in memory and you need to restart in order for them to be recognized. the choices for the `exe` command need to refresh after.

gotcha. i noticed in the code that even if one has personal routines, it would still display the default routines.

the last 3 lines are out of the conditional block so when the `if` clause runs, it will also display the default routines afterward."
reasoning_claude: This is a state management issue within a single component where cached data is not being refreshed properly. The bug can be reliably reproduced and validated by testing the account list service logic with controlled state scenarios.
evidence_claude: The routines are not refreshed in memory and you need to restart in order for them to be recognized. The last 3 lines are out of the conditional block so when the if clause runs, it will also display the default routines afterward.

text:
[title] [bug] /stocks/sia - load function doesn't work. error: 'nonetype' object is not subscriptable
[issue]
recreate by entering the menu with no stock loaded, then load a ticker from any source.
[issue_comments] "@deeleeramone i had a quick look at this and it looks like it's **intermittent**. the first time i loaded i got the same error (it makes no difference if it's loaded before going to `sia` or after).

the reason is that we load this data from yahoo finance and the data (which they call `summaryprofile` was missing altogether).

making it not crash with that error is trivial, but my main question is how should we report this kind of errors? i am very new to this project, but it does look like that menu cannot be used unless we know the sector, country and industry.

a good solution would be to find an alternative source if we can't find it in yahoo finance, can someone help me understand if we have an alternative source implemented?", "hmmm, @catalintoma i think you are right. we are relying on the sector, industry and country from yahoo finance. this is also because this menu utilizes financedatabase which is built on top of yahoo finance.

so i think it is not trivial to use another source in here, unfortunately", "has this been fixed? i can't seem to reproduce it.", "> has this been fixed? i can't seem to reproduce it.

as i wrote in the first comment, it is **intermittent**
i was able to reproduce for 2-3 times, basically that quote endpoint from yf returns
`summaryprofile: {}` which gets deserialized to `none`, hence the error message.

i want to improve the error message and refactor some duplicate code in that controller tomorrow morning, but as @didierrlopes said we don't have an alternative source, so not that much we can do.", "sounds good! i was just wanting to replace the nonetype error with a message that is more readable to a non coder."
reasoning_claude: This is an intermittent failure caused by external dependency behavior (Yahoo Finance API returning empty summaryProfile data). The issue is environment-dependent, non-deterministic, and occurs only when the third-party service returns incomplete data, making reliable automation impractical.
evidence_claude: The bug is described as 'intermittent' and occurs when 'that quote endpoint from yf returns summaryprofile: {} which gets deserialized to none'. The external API behavior is unpredictable and cannot be reliably reproduced.

This can be validated through isolated service-level tests that verify data flow between components with controlled inputs.

text:
[title] 1.26 regression: account listings with umlaut are now empty
[issue]
a second regression i noticed in 1.26: when an account name includes a german umlaut, the account page is now empty. for example:

`

both the table and the charts are empty. the heading (blue bar at the top) shows `aufw%c3%a4nde:30-haushalt` instead of `aufwände:30-haushalt` as the account name. maybe the account name is just url-encoded too early. or it’s never url-decoded.

also, on the balance sheet and income statement, these accounts show up just fine with their correct numbers. only the `/account/` page appears to be broken.

i have tested an account named "jahresüberschuss" and two whole classes of accounts starting with "erträge:" and "aufwände:". when i remove the ü/ä from the account names, fava works fine.

version 1.25 did not have this problem. the dev tools console in firefox shows no messages. i have just verifed the bug with fava 1.25 and fava 1.26 right after each other with the same firefox, so apparently it’s not browser behavior.
[issue_comments] "i can confirm this as well; my accounts containing swedish letters 'åäö' is now broken in the account page.", "same here using åäö in account names. tested in several different browsers in macos, ios and windows with the same result. downgraded back to 1.25 and the problem is gone.", "i managed to get the account reports working by changing this line. or rather the corresponding line in the compiled .js-file. where the account variable was actually called account2... i won't even pretend to understand how web development works :sweat_smile:

from:
```
      a: account,
```
to:
```
      a: decodeuri(account),
```

this gets the account page working again, but the account name is still url-encoded in the heading as @mjochim described, so this is not the complete fix, but hopefully it helps someone with better understanding of this stuff to come up with a proper fix.", "@fredrik-eriksson based on what you did i found out how to fix both the heading and the journal. i know how web development works, in general, but i’m not familiar with svelte. also, i don't know if @yagebu has any plans of bigger changes to this code, but i’ll prepare a pr and we’ll see if he likes it.", "thanks for the report and for providing the fix :)"
reasoning_claude: This is a character encoding regression in URL parameter handling within a single component (account page). The bug is isolated to how account names with special characters are encoded/decoded in the account service endpoint, and can be reliably tested with controlled input data without requiring full system integration.
evidence_claude: The heading shows `aufw%c3%a4nde:30-haushalt` instead of `aufwände:30-haushalt` as the account name. maybe the account name is just url-encoded too early. or it's never url-decoded. The fix was changing `a: account` to `a: decodeuri(account)` in the compiled .js-file.

text:
[title] filtering for "paid invoices" produces no results
[issue]
**describe the bug**

the `paid` invoices filter doesn't work anymore. all other invoice filters still work as expected (invalid, paid late, paid partial, paid over, unusual, archived).

this worked in all versions up to 1.3.7 and broke in 1.4.0.

when filtering for "paid", the filter query will change to `status:processing,status:settled` as defined here:

either this code is wrong or the documentation/explanation is, as per here:

my current workaround is to manually enter `status:complete` which will show all paid (settled) invoices.

**to reproduce the bug**
steps to reproduce the reported bug:
1. go to 'invoices'
2. click on 'paid invoices', via the drop-down menu on the right side of the search button
3. observe an empty list

**expected behavior**

i would expect paid invoices to show up, as they did in previous versions.

**screenshots**

(i removed the storeid when taking the screenshots above, but it doesn't matter)

**your btcpay environment (please complete the following information):**
- btcpay server version:     v1.4.3.0
- deployment method:        docker
- browser:                  firefox
[issue_comments] "thanks for reporting this, we no longer have `paid` status as of 1.4.0. paid is now called `processing` and we should definitely update our ui to reflect that.", "for what it's worth, `status:processing` doesn't produce any results either, so this might be a bug in the model or the logic, not purely in the ui.", "the state shown in the list is the new label and it looks like the filter is still applied based on the old status labels. i can reproduce it and filter the invoices using the old labels:

- `paid` (old) -> `processing` (new)
- `completed` (old) -> `settled` (new)
- `confirmed` (old) -> `settled` (new)

fixing this now.", "fix in #3436, as a workaround `status:paid,status:confirmed,status:complete` can be used.", "use `status:paid,status:confirmed,status:complete,status:settled,status:processing` instead.
this is fixed in next release."
reasoning_claude: This is a straightforward filter logic bug in the invoice service layer where the UI filter mapping to status values became misaligned after a status label refactoring. The issue can be reliably tested at the service level by validating that filter queries correctly map to the expected invoice statuses without requiring full system integration.
evidence_claude: The filter query changes to 'status:processing,status:settled' but these don't match the actual status values. The workaround 'status:paid,status:confirmed,status:complete' works, indicating a simple mapping issue in the filter logic that can be tested in isolation.

text:
[title] do not allow invoice `cancellation` for `payment requests` when "allow payee to create invoices in their own denomination" is not enabled
[issue]
**describe the bug**

the payment request can be easily canceled, which is a bit out of ordinary behavior because such an invoice is marked as `invalid marked`. if a user is used to seeing `invalid` status in their invoices view, they may make a mistake of labeling it as `complete` thinking the payment request is paid but has not received confirmation on the blockchain on time.

this is indeed an edge-case, but due to this unexpected error, a mistake can happen. i've talked to @kukks about this and we agreed this shouldn't happen.

the proposal is to disable `payment request cancelation` unless `allow payee to create invoices in their own denomination` is enabled on the payment request. the reason behind this is to allow payee who may have created a wrong amount to `cancel the invoice`

**to reproduce the bug**
steps to reproduce the reported bug:
1. create a payment request
2. click `pay`button
3. click `cancel invoice`
4. go to `invoices` list and see the payment request invoice marked as `invalid`

### logs

interestingly enough this also throws an error in logs

```
021-08-23 08:18:04.491 +00:00 [inf] btc: new block

system.nullreferenceexception: object reference not set to an instance of an object.
   at btcpayserver.paymentrequest.paymentrequesthub.pay(nullable`1 amount) in /source/btcpayserver/paymentrequest/paymentrequesthub.cs:line 52
   at microsoft.aspnetcore.signalr.internal.defaulthubdispatcher`1.executehubmethod(objectmethodexecutor methodexecutor, thub hub, object[] arguments)
   at microsoft.aspnetcore.signalr.internal.defaulthubdispatcher`1.<>c__displayclass13_0.g__executeinvocation|0>d.movenext()

```
[issue_comments] "i wonder if it would make sense to include an additional status here so that if a user cancels a payment request it's marked as "cancelled" instead of "invalid"? or would that be too much just for this one case?", "haha please let's not due that, the statuses we have are already mind-boggling for most users. since this is an edge-case simply not allowing it makes sense to me so concept ack on #2843"
reasoning_claude: This bug involves validating business rules around payment request cancellation based on a specific configuration flag. The logic can be tested in isolation by verifying that the cancellation action is properly blocked when the denomination setting is disabled, without requiring full system integration.
evidence_claude: The proposal is to disable payment request cancelation unless allow payee to create invoices in their own denomination is enabled on the payment request. The NullReferenceException at paymentrequesthub.pay indicates a service-level logic error that can be caught through component testing.

[title] issue with phase transition for account in local tz
[issue]
consider the following scenario:

`tz=america/new_york`, ref time=`2023-03-01 03:47:56 utc` (`2023-02-28 22:47:56 ny`) and bcd= 28.

given a plan with 2 phases, a 3 months discount, then an evergreen, we expect the following:
* invoice 1: 2023-02-28 -> 2023-03-28 (discount)
* invoice 2: 2023-03-28 -> 2023-04-28 (discount)
* invoice 3: 2023-04-28 -> 2023-05-28 (discount)
* invoice 3: 2023-05-28 -> 2023-06-28 (evergreen)

however we observe a pro-ration after invoice 3 to re-align from `2023-05-28` -> `2023-06-01` because the computation of the phase effective date is incorrect (does not take into consideration the local tz.
reasoning_claude: This is a date calculation logic defect within the billing service's phase transition algorithm that fails to account for timezone offsets when computing billing cycle dates. The issue can be reliably reproduced with controlled inputs (specific timezone, reference time, and billing cycle day) and validated through isolated service-level tests with mocked time providers.
evidence_claude: The computation of the phase effective date is incorrect (does not take into consideration the local tz). Given specific inputs (tz=america/new_york, ref time=2023-03-01 03:47:56 utc, bcd=28), the system incorrectly calculates phase transitions, producing unexpected pro-ration behavior.

text:
[title] usage points at cancellation day not taken into account
[issue]
in kill bill, by convention, the upper limit of an interval is always open. in practice, this means that when we bill from 2021-07-01 to 2021-08-01, we effectively bill for [2021-07-01, 2021-08-01), i.e. the last day being billed is 2021-07-31.

if a subscription starts at 3pm on 2021-07-01, we effectively bill for [2021-07-01t15:00:00, 2021-08-01t15:00:00), even though the invoice doesn't reflect the hour of the day.

when rolling up the raw usages, we are currently ignoring the last day, so any usages between [2021-08-01t00:00:00, 2021-08-01t15:00:00) are discarded (while these should be billed).
reasoning_claude: This is a logic defect in usage aggregation within a billing service component. The issue involves incorrect interval boundary handling when rolling up raw usage data, which can be validated in isolation with controlled test data and mocked dependencies.
evidence_claude: when rolling up the raw usages, we are currently ignoring the last day, so any usages between [2021-08-01t00:00:00, 2021-08-01t15:00:00) are discarded (while these should be billed).

text:
[title] reader cannot consume large, single-line ach files
[issue]
### ach version

master

### what were you trying to do?

we sometimes receive large ach files where:

1. the individual 94 byte records are not separated by newlines
2. the entire file exceeds the `bufio.maxtokensize`

 `ach.reader` runs into problems parsing the file because it uses a `scanner` that splits on newlines, and has a 64k maximum buffer/token size.

because `ach.reader.read` doesn't actually check the result of `scanner.scan()` (or `scanner.err()`), it attempts to parse an arbitrary 64k chunk of data as an ach record (or series of records) and fails.

### what did you expect to see?

`ach.reader` should be able to consume arbitrarily large ach files, with or without newline-delimited records (at least until we run out of memory).

i think using a custom `splitfunc` (that breaks 94 byte records and/or newlines -- to handle "broken" lines that aren't padded to 94 characters) for the `scanner` instead of the default `scanlines` would be more reliable.

### what did you see?

ach.reader fails to properly parse the file with some cryptic message about

### how can we reproduce the problem?

attempt to read an ach file that:

1. is not newline-delimited
2. exceeds 64k bytes (`bufio.maxscantokensize`)
[issue_comments] "i've opened  to fix this bug. can you try out the branch on your file and see if parsing works?", "yes, that mr seems to do the trick, thanks for the quick fix!", "fix released in"
reasoning_claude: This is a file parsing logic defect in the ACH reader component that can be validated in isolation with controlled test inputs. The fix involves changing the scanner's split function to handle 94-byte records correctly, which can be reliably tested at the service level with synthetic files of various sizes.
evidence_claude: ach.reader runs into problems parsing the file because it uses a scanner that splits on newlines, and has a 64k maximum buffer/token size. Using a custom splitfunc that breaks 94 byte records would be more reliable.

text:
[title] refactor unit tests
[issue]
most (all?) of the unit tests in wire put their assertions inside conditional blocks. these tests trigger errors by deliberately crafting invalid tags and/or messages, but they pass if no error is returned. for example:
```go
	if err := pn.validate(); err != nil {
		if !base.match(err, errvalidtagfortype) {
			t.errorf("%t: %s", err, err)
		}
	}
```

the tests should be refactored to fail if no error is returned. the test above should be
```go
	err := pn.validate()

	if err == nil {
		t.fatalf("an error was expected - wanted %t, %s", errvalidtagfortype, errvalidtagfortype)
	}
	if !base.match(err, errvalidtagfortype) {
		t.errorf("%t: %s", err, err)
	}
```

better yet, the tests could be improved by using the testify package to `assert` or `require` test results:
```go
	err := pn.validate()

	require.notnil(t, err)
	require.contains(t, err.error(), errvalidtagfortype.error())
```
[issue_comments] "yes please. let's switch to testify."
reasoning_claude: This is a code quality improvement task focused on refactoring existing unit tests to improve their reliability and readability. It does not describe a functional defect that could be prevented by automated testing, but rather identifies technical debt in the test suite itself.
evidence_claude: The issue states 'the tests should be refactored to fail if no error is returned' and 'the tests could be improved by using the testify package' - this is a development task to improve test code quality, not a bug requiring test coverage.
